---
title: "Yellow Bachelor's Button (Senega rugelii)"
slug: "senega-rugelii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476061152/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Yellow Bachelor's Button"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Fabales"
tax_family: "Polygalaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Fabales", "Polygalaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476061152/medium.jpg" alt="Yellow Bachelor's Button" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/lmcconachie">
lmcconachie</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/265120906">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Fabales
-   **Family:** Polygalaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1568931)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry to mesic sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small wildflower characterized by its narrow leaves and clusters of tiny yellowish-green flowers arranged in short spikes. In Florida, it is found in dry to mesic sandy habitats such as flatwoods, sandhills, and open pine woodlands. It grows during the warmer months and is often a subtle component of groundcover vegetation in fire-maintained ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/122054-Woodwardia-virginica)
-   [Local profile](https://wildflowersearch.org/search?tsn=29312)
